scope_compliance: This side-agent packet only writes under Documentation/Planning/V0_1_33_BatchPlan/.
batch_posture: visible milestone batch, not a one-prop release

parent_lane_context.v0.1.33_start_rule: Begin implementation only after the parent lane completes or intentionally supersedes v0.1.32.

batch_goals: Ship a broad 10-family environment/route-dressing milestone in one version.
Make pressure gates, service lifts, route valves, hoists, route returns, and final thresholds visibly heavier and more authored.
Improve route comprehension through material language and placement rhythm without new gameplay prompts or route logic.
Keep all components Unity-only through primitives, procedural mesh helpers, generated quads/decals, ProBuilder-style geometry if already present, and existing in-project materials.
Preserve existing gameplay authority with no route-state ownership, triggers, pickup authority, transition logic, enemy blockers, damage behavior, or nav obstacles.
Use targeted validation during development and run one route audit plus one full V0 matrix after the batch is coherent.

not_acceptable: any gameplay-authority transfer from existing route systems

components.role: Heavy side braces at pressure gates, lift thresholds, hoists, and final route thresholds.
components.route_safe_contract: Wall/threshold-adjacent visual geometry only; center aperture remains clear; no colliders or gameplay components.

components.role: Extra pipe collars, flanges, short couplers, and clamp bands around existing route pipework.
components.minimum_target: 8 grouped placements across at least 4 levels
components.route_safe_contract: Attach to existing wall, ceiling, or pipe dressing; no new pipe crosses walkable space.

components.role: Flat oil, soot, scorch, leak, and worn-edge panels near machinery, valve consoles, lift motors, and threshold seams.
components.preferred_target: 15 grouped placements, at least 3 per level
components.route_safe_contract: Flush decals, quads, or paper-thin panels; no collision and no obstruction of prompts, pickups, hazards, enemies, or route-state cues.

components.role: Small amber route-attention plates near pressure locks, valves, lifts, restored paths, and route returns.
components.route_safe_contract: Diegetic status dressing only; subtle emissive material if needed; no prompts, buttons, or new dynamic lights.

components.role: Thin brass and blackened-iron sill or wall kick plates at repeated route thresholds.
components.minimum_target: 5 threshold grouped placements across all levels
components.route_safe_contract: Flush or near-flush trim with maximum visual rise target of 0.03m; no colliders, cover, ramps, or snag geometry.

components.role: Small bolted repair plates and stamped metal patches on route-adjacent wall panels.
components.route_safe_contract: Wall-bound noninteractive dressing only; no signage objective, route blocker, prompt, or gameplay component.

components.role: Rubberized or dark iron gasket rings and seal bands around locked gates, lift frames, and heavy machinery seams.
components.route_safe_contract: Surface-mounted dressing; does not change door frames, route apertures, or transition volumes.

components.role: Short capped pipe stubs, elbow markers, and brass return-flow tags that reinforce where pressure has been routed.
components.route_safe_contract: Uses existing route-state color language; no new interaction or objective logic.

components.role: Soot-dark vent collars, heat halo plates, and condensation or oil marks near existing steam, furnace, and relief machinery.
components.route_safe_contract: Visual residue only; no steam hazards, particles, damage, or visibility-blocking effects.

components.role: Wall or ceiling hoist anchor plates, chain brackets, pulley backers, and riveted mounts near lift and hoist thresholds.
components.route_safe_contract: Visual mount dressing only; no physics chains, collision, moving platforms, or transition authority.

acceptance_gates: Implementation visibly reads as a milestone route-dressing pass, not a single asset promotion.
Preferred ship target is all 10 families; minimum acceptable target is 8 families if targeted validation proves crowding.
TRD-001 through TRD-006 are preserved in any reduced scope.
At least 2 of TRD-007 through TRD-010 ship if the batch is reduced to 8 families.
Level01 through Level05 each receive at least 7 grouped placements.
Piston braces appear on at least 3 current route thresholds and preferably all 5 listed threshold roles.
Root names contain exact component names and placement roles.
Metadata records component name, placement role, promotion version v0.1.33, and batch ID v0.1.33_threshold_route_dressing_milestone.
Named hierarchy exposes major material and detail roles.
Material-role coverage includes blackened iron, aged brass, copper or pipe metal where relevant, amber glass or enamel where relevant, dark hardware, oil or soot or scorch wear, and worn edges.

No component hierarchy contains colliders, trigger colliders, NavMeshObstacle, route-state controllers, interactables, pickup scripts, damage scripts, transition scripts, objective scripts, physics chain components, or prompt ownership.
Amber plates and lamps remain diegetic and do not imply buttons, quest markers, or new required interactions.
Existing route triggers, pickups, locked gates, lift transitions, Warden defeat gating, enemy placement, weapon pickups, and hazard behavior remain authoritative and unchanged.
Route audit reports no blockers after placement.
Level validation checks expected roles, material roles, detail counts, metadata, and zero-collider/no-authority constraints.
Targeted route smoke tests pass before the full V0 matrix.
One full V0 matrix passes after the complete batch is assembled and tuned.

checks: Find each expected grouped root by exact object name.
Confirm metadata component, component name, placement role, promotion version, and milestone batch ID.
Confirm named child groups for structural plates, pipes or clamps, indicators, grime, fasteners, threshold trim, gasket or seal, return marker, vent residue, and hoist anchor roles as relevant.
Count renderer-bearing children for required part types.
Confirm required material-role names through materials, renderer names, child names, or metadata.
Confirm zero colliders, zero trigger colliders, zero NavMeshObstacle, and zero gameplay authority components.
Confirm piston brace clearance markers or measured named bounds keep the route aperture unobstructed.
Confirm amber, green, and red state language is consistent with existing route state.
Confirm no dressing root is placed inside known transition, pickup, hazard, enemy, or boss-combat ownership volumes.
Confirm milestone family count and per-level grouped placement count meet batch gates.

targeted_tests_before_full_matrix: Compile/editor validation after Compile A foundation families.
Compile/editor validation after Compile B route language families.
Compile/editor validation after Compile C threshold mass families and generated scene rebuild.
Level validation for Level01 through Level05 with milestone dressing checks enabled.
Targeted Level01 smoke: spawn to key gate to service lift.
Targeted Level02 smoke: locked Boilerheart lift, routing valve, restored lift route, and Lancer lane visibility.
Targeted Level03 smoke: pressure valve, steam hazard lanes, scattergun pickup readability, Bellows Node space, and Foundry lift approach.
Targeted Level04 smoke: furnace hazard lanes, Bulwark combat footprint, secret-cache approach, and emergency hoist threshold.
Targeted Level05 smoke: Warden arena movement, locked final hoist, Warden defeat unlock, and final route readability.
Focused first-person visual review at each piston brace and each dense amber-plate cluster.
Route audit after targeted smoke passes.
One full V0 build matrix after the batch is stable.
implementation_order: Confirm v0.1.32 is complete or intentionally superseded.
Add shared milestone batch ID, role registry, grouped placement table, and validator scaffolding.
Implement foundation families in one leap: grime panels, riveted repair plates, pipe clamps/couplers.
Implement route language families in one leap: amber plates, brass threshold kick plates, seal/gasket rings, return pipe markers.
Implement threshold mass families in one leap: piston door braces, steam vent residue collars, hoist chain anchor plates.
Rebuild Level01 through Level05 and run level validation.
Run targeted route smokes and first-person visual review.
Remove or thin grouped placements that crowd route readability, hazard tells, pickup readability, combat movement, or final-hoist clarity.
Run route audit and one full V0 matrix.
Refresh release notes, candidate readiness, work ledger, roadmap, and session log in the main lane after the verified milestone batch is complete.
side_agent_boundaries: This side agent writes only inside Documentation/Planning/V0_1_33_BatchPlan/.
Do not edit Assets, Tools, generated scenes, build scripts, release docs, shared status docs, or Git history from this side-agent scope.
Parent/main lane owns Unity implementation, validators, generated scene files, test evidence, package artifacts, releases, commits, and pushes.
Future side agents should use disjoint folders or file sets and prepare batch-ready briefs, acceptance gates, material recipes, or QA checklists.
Do not revert or overwrite active v0.1.32 work or unrelated user changes.
